Title: X users report platform glitch, say video sharing impossible
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: X (formerly Twitter) users have reported problems with the platform's video link copy feature since March 31, with many saying it no longer works.
According to International Business Times, users attempting to share video clips, including sports highlights and viral content, said videos now auto-play instead of generating direct links.
The issue has sparked frustration, with users saying it is harder to share content or embed videos on other platforms.
The problem appears to affect a wide range of users, although some still have access to the feature, raising questions over whether it is an intentional change or a technical glitch.
Users have also reported missing or altered features, including circles, audio tweets and GIF history, which are either unavailable or not functioning as expected.
Sports-related content has drawn strong criticism, as users rely on quick sharing of highlights during live events.
The issue has also affected memes and short-form videos, impacting both content creators and general users.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakistan says onus on Afghanistan to end hostilities
Article:
ISLAMABAD: Pakistan said Thursday that the burden of ending its conflict with Afghanistan lay with Kabul, as the two sides held preliminary talks to try to end hostilities.
The neighbours and one-time allies have been locked in an escalating conflict over claims from Islamabad that Kabul is harbouring extremists responsible for cross-border attacks.
Negotiations started in Urumqi, in northwest China, on Wednesday, after Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar travelled to Beijing for talks with his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi.
Pakistan foreign ministry spokesman Tahir Hussain Andrabi told reporters in the capital that the government hoped for a "durable solution."
"Our participation (in talks) is a reiteration of our core concerns," he said.
"The burden of real process, however, lies with Afghanistan, which must demonstrate visible and verifiable actions against terrorist groups using (its) soil against Pakistan."
He described the negotiations as "working level talks" and said they were ongoing.
"Our delegation has not returned yet," he added.
Pakistan, which has longstanding ties with Iran and personal contacts with the Trump administration, has in recent weeks been engaged in a flurry of diplomacy to try to bring Washington and Teheran to the negotiating table to end their own war in the Middle East.
China has backed Pakistan's efforts, aligning itself with the aims of Gulf countries affected by the spread of the conflict in the region.
Beijing has also been involved in trying to end the conflict with Afghanistan, and has sent a special envoy to Kabul to try to broker a deal.
Pakistan and Afghanistan announced a pause in fighting to mark the end of Ramadan last month, at the request of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye.
But sporadic attacks have been reported in remote border areas since the temporary truce ended.
The Taliban government denies providing sanctuary to extremists.
Saudi Arabia and Qatar have previously attempted to reduce tensions since the conflict escalated in February, while talks in Istanbul last November also failed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist's leg severed, another seriously injured in Jalan Sultan Ismail crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two motorcyclists were seriously injured, including one whose leg was severed, in a crash with a car at a traffic light junction along Jalan Sultan Ismail here early today.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incident, which involved a Honda City and two Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycles, occurred at 4.27am.
He said police received a report on the accident at 8am, while a video of the incident later went viral on social media platform Threads at 8.45am today.
"Preliminary investigations found that the two motorcycles, ridden by a 24-year-old and a 19-year-old, were crossing the junction on a green light when a car driven by a 25-year-old man ran a red light and collided with them," he said in a statement.
Both motorcyclists had serious injuries and were taken to Kuala Lumpur Hospital for treatment.
The 24-year-old victim suffered a severed left leg, while the 19-year-old sustained fractures to his left arm and left leg.
The car driver sustained minor injuries to his hand, while a passenger in the vehicle suffered a head injury.
Zamzuri said a 19-year-old witness lodged a police report and had his statement recorded.
The driver of the car was tracked down and arrested at 1pm at Klinik Jacob in Kundang, Rawang, where he was seeking treatment.
"An alcohol test conducted on the suspect found that it did not exceed the permitted limit, while a urine screening test is being carried out at the Dang Wangi police headquarters," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving.
Those found guilty under the section are liable to jail terms of up to five years and a fine of between RM5,000 and RM15,000 for the first offence.
Subsequent offences carry a jail term of up to 10 years and a fine of between RM10,000 and RM20,000.
Those with information can contact the Kuala Lumpur TEID headquarters at at 03-20719999 or any nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy crisis: Energy use in govt buildings tightened
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Energy controls in government buildings will be tightened, including setting air-conditioning temperatures at no lower than 24 degrees Celsius, following the global energy crisis, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said.
Fadillah, who also serves as energy transition and water transformation minister, urged civil servants to wear attire better suited to the local climate, such as batik, corporate shirts or baju Melayu, to reduce reliance on excessive cooling.
He said the measures reflect the government's commitment to leading by example in prudent energy use.
"As a proactive response to the global energy crisis, the government will implement several energy-saving measures at the national level," he said at the Global Energy Crisis Briefing, broadcast live by local television stations today.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced in a video message that a work-from-home policy will be introduced from April 15 for the public sector and government-linked companies.
The decision, made at a Cabinet meeting, aims to cut fuel consumption and ensure the sustainability of the nation's energy supply amid the ongoing global energy crisis.
Fadillah said Malaysia remains in a stable position but is ready to take proactive measures to manage the impact of the global energy crisis, triggered by conflict in Middle East that has disrupted supply and driven up oil prices.
"We are now in a challenging phase of the global economic landscape. The ongoing global energy crisis, following conflict in West Asia, has had a direct impact on energy supply and prices worldwide," he said.
He said that disruptions to key routes such as the Strait of Hormuz, which is a vital artery for global oil flows, have heightened market uncertainty and pushed prices higher.
As an open economy, Malaysia is not insulated from the fallout, he said.
"We must remain vigilant and take precautionary measures should the crisis persist," Fadillah said, adding that the government recognises public concern over the crisis, and that its response will be clear, coordinated and focused on key priorities.
-- Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Akmal: Government adopts proactive measures to tackle global energy crisis
Article:
NILAI: The government is taking a proactive approach in assessing the global energy crisis, focusing on medium-term planning to ensure Malaysia's economic stability remains strong and protected.
Economy Minister, Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, said the ministry expects the conflict to last at least six months, but initial plans have been drawn up to deal with any eventuality.
"The ministry expects that the conflict will last six months at the earliest. If not, the effects may extend to a year. That is why planning and preparation need to consider the medium term.
"So we are examining how we can intervene and ensure urgent priorities are addressed as effectively as possible," he told reporters after visiting the Malaysia Vision Valley (MVV) project site for the construction of the Nilai-Labu to Bandar Enstek road, attended by Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar, Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
Akmal was commenting on the prospects for the people if the conflict continues until mid-year. He added that monitoring at the ministry level is comprehensive, involving dashboards to examine data and statistics, as well as obtaining direct feedback from the industry.
He said that the global energy crisis has affected the supply chain dependent on diesel, oil and gas, forcing industries to seek alternative sources to ensure continuity of operations.
"The ministry is considering the medium and long-term economic impact. We want to ensure that the effects do not disrupt economic activities entirely.
"We also aim to maintain economic activity. In managing medium-term economic growth, the public's spending power reflects economic progress, alongside investment from the private sector or government spending on infrastructure and development," he said.
Akmal added that his ministry is addressing medium-term matters from a macro perspective, while other ministries and agencies assess the direct impacts in greater detail.
Regarding today's visit, he said the Nilai-Labu to Bandar Enstek road project involves a cost of RM557.5 million and is expected to be completed in the first quarter of next year.
"The visit was to review the progress of this federal government project, which aims to improve the road network and support the development of the area as a key growth corridor for investment, industry, logistics, housing and workforce movement.
"This project will indirectly benefit users and local communities through smoother travel, reduced congestion and better access to key economic areas in Negeri Sembilan," he said.
Akmal said that all parties need to strengthen coordination and expedite follow-up actions so that the project remains on schedule, does not affect economic momentum, and avoids additional development costs.
"In the current challenging circumstances, with the country increasingly feeling the effects of the global energy crisis, including uncertainty due to conflicts in the Middle East, we cannot allow delays.
"Any issues must be resolved quickly, in an integrated and pragmatic manner, so the project is completed on time and its benefits reach the people without delay," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia well-positioned amid global headwinds: Bank Negara deputy governor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The country is well-positioned to weather an increasingly challenging global economic landscape but must move decisively to seize opportunities arising from ongoing structural shifts, said Bank Negara Malaysia deputy governor Datuk Marzunisham Omar.
Malaysia's strong economic fundamentals, underpinned by resilient domestic demand, low inflation and a robust external position, continue to provide a key buffer against heightened global uncertainties, he added.
"Malaysia is entering this challenging period from a position of strength, with a diversified economy, stable domestic demand and a robust financial system," he said during the Bank Negara Malaysia Address on the Malaysian Economy & Panel Discussion here today.
Marzunisham said domestic growth momentum continues to be supported by private consumption and investment, as well as a firm labour market with low unemployment.
However, he stressed that these strengths must be further reinforced through structural reforms to ensure long-term economic resilience.
In the current context, he said the uncertain global environment requires Malaysia to enhance its adaptability, particularly in addressing supply chain disruptions and shifts in global trade patterns.
"Global supply chains are no longer driven solely by efficiency, but are increasingly focused on resilience. This requires Malaysia to reassess its trade and investment strategies."
Marzunisham added that efforts to diversify trading partners and strengthen regional integration, particularly within Asean, should be intensified to reduce reliance on specific markets.
At the same time, he emphasised that Malaysia's participation in global value chains remains significant and should be further enhanced by strengthening domestic industrial capabilities.
On current risks, he said conflicts in the Middle East have a direct impact on Malaysia's economy through higher commodity prices, trade disruptions and financial market uncertainties.
"We are already seeing pressure on energy and other commodity prices, as well as disruptions to global trade flows that could affect the country's exports," he said.
He noted that these impacts are transmitted through three main channels, namely prices, trade and finance, each affecting inflation, growth and market stability.
"In facing these challenges, the monetary policy approach needs to be cautious, as current shocks are largely supply-driven. Not all inflation shocks require a monetary policy response. If they are temporary, we will look through the initial impact on inflation," he said.
He noted that Bank Negara will continue to monitor factors such as cost pass-through to consumers, second-round effects on wages and inflation expectations in determining policy direction.
He also said the strength of the domestic financial sector ensures continued financing to households and businesses even under challenging conditions.
"Our financial institutions remain strong and capable of supporting economic activity, including small and medium enterprises," he said.
Meanwhile, Marzunisham emphasised the importance of raising incomes as a core pillar of sustainable growth, rather than focusing solely on maintaining low inflation.
"Price stability is important, but it is not sufficient. We must ensure that incomes grow sustainably," he said.
He added that efforts to attract high-quality investments that create high-paying jobs should be intensified, alongside improving workforce skills.
On the labour market, Marzunisham also highlighted the issue of "wage compression", which could undermine incentives for skills upgrading and productivity growth.
Heightened geopolitical developments are increasingly influencing economic outcomes, with financial markets already reacting negatively to recent global policy signals.
He added that global trade is becoming more fragmented as policy decisions are shaped by broader strategic considerations beyond economics.
Marzunisham said the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is already affecting global conditions through tighter financial markets, disrupted trade flows and rising uncertainty.
He noted that these developments are weighing on sentiment among households and businesses globally.
"In this environment, it is very difficult to do economic forecasting, but as a central bank, we must remain in a state of preparedness and readiness," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Adopt pragmatic approach to tourism subsidy
Article:
LETTER:
The Transport Ministry needs to take serious concern over the rising cost of tourism transportation following the exclusion of tour buses from subsidies under the Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS 2.0).
The tourism industry remains one of the key drivers of the national economy.
However, when transportation costs soar due to higher fuel prices and the absence of support such as subsidies for tour buses, this will directly raise the overall cost of travel packages.
This situation will make Malaysia less competitive compared to other destinations in the region.
Tour buses are not limited to carrying international; they also play an important role in transporting domestic travellers and tour groups to destinations in the northern and southern regions, as well as along the East Coast of Peninsular Malaysia.
While it is true that these buses largely serve foreign tourists, their contribution to domestic tourism is equally important, especially during long school holiday periods.
Yesterday, the media reported that the retail price of diesel in Peninsular Malaysia increased by 50 sen to RM6.02 per litre, up from RM5.52 previously.
Uncertain global conditions, including international conflicts, have contributed to rising oil prices, placing additional pressure on the local tourism industry.
Without support for tourism transportation, costs will continue to rise. As costs increase, tourists may choose more affordable destinations elsewhere, which would undermine the government's efforts to promote Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026).
The tourism sector reflects a Chinese proverb which means "praised when successful, criticised when it fails."
This is the reality. When tourism generates strong revenue from high visitor arrivals, it is applauded; but when the situation declines, the industry is treated like a "stepchild."
This is the challenge the tourism sector faces today. If the government does not provide support to the tourism sector, the impact will not be limited to tourism companies alone.
It will create a ripple effect across related industries such as hospitality, food and beverage (F&B), retail, and services. Without tourist arrivals, there will be no spending inflow into the local economy, which will ultimately affect national economic growth.
In this regard, MCA, through its Tourism Bureau, urges the government to review its subsidy policies and adopt a more pragmatic and mature approach in addressing this issue.
Failure to do so risks causing significant losses to the national tourism industry and jeopardising the objectives of Visit Malaysia 2026.
Cooperation from all parties is crucial to ensure that the tourism industry remains competitive and continues to be a major contributor to the national economy.
LAWRENCE CHIEW KAI HENG
Chairman
MCA Tourism Bureau
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proton expects to hit RM30bil in vendor spend by 2030
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd expects to continue supporting its vendor ecosystem with procurement projected to reach RM30 billion over the next five years, reinforcing its role in strengthening Malaysia's automotive industry.
The national carmaker, a subsidiary of DRB-HICOM Bhd, said the commitment builds on more than RM17 billion in local procurement recorded between 2021 and 2025.
Proton said it remains focused on ensuring the local automotive sector stays relevant and competitive amid rapid technological shifts, in line with the government's broader mobility agenda.
The company said it has undertaken significant investments to enhance its capabilities, including expanding vehicle and powertrain manufacturing capacity in Tanjung Malim, Perak.
This includes the local assembly of key electrified components such as electric drive units and dedicated hybrid transmissions to support its growing range of hybrid and electric models.
Proton chairman Tan Sri Syed Faisal Albar said it continues to be guided by its founding purpose of building national capability while supporting long-term industrial aspirations.
"Through close collaboration with the government and industry stakeholders, Proton will continue to play its role in ensuring Malaysia's automotive industry remains relevant and competitive as it evolves," he added.
The company has also established Malaysia's first dedicated electric vehicle (EV) assembly plant, strengthening its position in the country's new energy vehicle push.
These efforts are anchored by the Automotive High Tech Valley development, where Proton plays a central role in advancing NEVs, talent development and research.
The initiative is expected to attract foreign investments while boosting Malaysia's competitiveness in future mobility.
Proton said its EV rollout has gained traction, helping build consumer confidence and support the national electrification agenda.
The group is also expanding its export footprint, currently present in 18 markets, with plans to double this over the next five years.
"Looking ahead, Proton remains committed to supporting the continued growth and development of Malaysia's automotive industry," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar urges legal profession to rethink role in AI era
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on the legal profession to undergo a fundamental shift in values, skills and knowledge to remain relevant in the era of artificial intelligence (AI) and rapid global change.
Speaking at the inaugural Commonwealth Legal Education Association (CLEA) Conference, he said the future of lawyering must go beyond mastering statutes and precedents, as these are increasingly becoming the domain of advanced AI systems.
"The lawyer of 2026 will no longer be a 'search engine in a suit' because far more will be required.
"What needs to be cultivated today must shift to those that are not easily replicated and are complementary to current and upcoming AI capabilities," he said when officiating the conference themed Lawyering 2026 – Skills, Strategy & Legal Education at Universiti Malaya here today.
He said law students must be trained to act as ethical filters in an increasingly complex data environment.
"The lawyer of 2026 and beyond must be competent in technology and AI, able to make sense of data, and imbued with a culture of ensuring that the law is used for the public good," he added.
Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, CLEA patron Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan, Universiti Malaya pro-chancellor Tan Sri Zainun Ali and vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Seri Dr Noor Azuan Abu Osman.
Anwar emphasised that values and ethics must remain central to legal development amid rapid technological change, with the Madani framework prioritising compassion, dignity and moral responsibility.
He said lawyers must serve as ethical authorities and moral anchors, ensuring that legal outcomes are not only correct but also just, while demonstrating empathy in handling disputes.
He added that this shift would enable a more holistic approach to dispute resolution, including mediation, negotiation and conciliation, particularly in domestic and private law matters.
"Digital assets and climate change have redefined the types of legal issues and solutions required. The lawyer of the future must be able to navigate the laws of multiple jurisdictions as easily as their own," he said.
For current practitioners, Anwar stressed the need for continuous adaptation to keep pace with global developments.
"It bears remembering that the law facilitates the day-to-day living of the citizen. It is necessary to comprehend the needs of the public, and not to gate-keep the law within a gilded cage that makes it inaccessible to the common man," he said.
On Malaysia's reform agenda, he said the government remains committed to improving access to justice, particularly for marginalised communities, in line with the Malaysia Madani concept.
While progress has been made, he said more efforts are needed to achieve what he described as "legal singularity" — a state where every citizen has meaningful access to justice and remedies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: VTAREC: Focus on risky behaviour, not just drink-driving, to cut road deaths
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Road fatalities in Malaysia are driven largely by human error, risky behaviour and systemic safety gaps rather than alcohol-impaired driving alone, according to the Vehicle Theft and Accident Reduction Council of Malaysia (VTAREC).
Its coordinator, Mas Tina Abdul Hamid, said the council recently compiled a report showing that while drunk driving often dominates public discourse, it represents only a small portion of overall road deaths.
"Drink-driving represents only a small fraction of Malaysia's road fatalities.
"To make Malaysian roads truly safer, we must broaden our focus beyond alcohol and base our strategy on data, impact and prevention," she said, adding that Malaysia continues to record persistently high accident rates, with 598,635 accidents and 6,433 fatalities in 2023, averaging about 18 deaths daily.
She said motorcyclists remain the most vulnerable group, accounting for about 65 per cent of fatalities.
"Preliminary figures for 2024 showed a similar trend, with 477,416 accidents and 4,844 deaths recorded up to September.
"A deeper analysis of accident causes shows that most fatal crashes stem from human error and high-risk behaviour," she said.
She said that human error, risky behaviours and systemic road safety deficiencies are the main drivers of fatal crashes, rather than alcohol consumption alone.
"Police data cited in the report showed alcohol-related fatalities accounted for less than 0.5 per cent of total road deaths annually, with 14 cases in 2022, 13 in 2023, 12 in 2024 and two in the first half of 2025.
"Despite this, enforcement against drunk driving remains necessary but should not overshadow broader safety priorities.
"Focusing solely on drink-driving does not address the primary determinants of mortality on Malaysian roads," she said.
She said the report also highlighted the economic impact of road accidents, estimating losses of about RM25 billion in 2023 due to healthcare costs, lost productivity and property damage.
Mas Tina said Malaysia must adopt a more holistic and data-driven approach, including stronger enforcement against high-risk behaviours, improved infrastructure and sustained public education.
"Road safety is not merely a legal or administrative concern. It is a public health imperative and a moral obligation," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police nab man over series of gold shop thefts, RM50k bracelets seized
Article:
PENAMPANG: A man suspected of involvement in a series of thefts at jewellery shops around Penampang and Putatan was arrested by police on Tuesday.
The 45-year-old local suspect was detained by a team from the Penampang district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Division at 1pm at a commercial building in Kota Kinabalu.
Penampang district police chief, Superintendent Sammy Newton, said further investigations led to the seizure of six gold bracelets of various sizes.
"The total value of the seized items is estimated at RM50,000 based on market prices.
"A vehicle, mobile phone, clothing and related documents were also seized to assist in further investigations," he said in a statement today.
Checks revealed that the suspect has several prior records involving property crimes and drug abuse.
The case is being investigated under Section 380 of the Penal Code, which carries a jail term of up to 10 years, a fine, and whipping for repeat offenders.
Meanwhile, he urged the public to continue providing information on criminal or suspicious activities to the police.
He said such cooperation is vital in ensuring public safety and maintaining order.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fix rice supply, not just stockpile, says researcher
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Malaysia must move beyond expanding rice stockpiles and address structural weaknesses in its food system as global disruptions intensify.
Legislative researcher John Ooh Sin Hwa said raising reserves to nine months offered only short-term relief.
Ooh, a former Penang city councillor and ex-secretary-general of the Rural Industry Entrepreneur Organisation, warned that the measure does not resolve deeper vulnerabilities in Malaysia's food security.
The Middle East conflict and blockade at the Strait of Hormuz are disrupting global supply chains, driving up freight, fuel and fertiliser costs.
"Although Malaysians remain cushioned by subsidised fuel, reliance on imported rice leaves the country exposed to external shocks," he told the New Straits Times today.
Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu recently announced an increase in
stockpiles
from six to nine months.
Ooh cautioned that the move risks becoming a costly stopgap rather than a sustainable solution.
Malaysia's rice self-sufficiency ratio (SSR) has declined to 56.2 per cent, well below the 75 per cent target under the National Agrofood Policy 2.0.
With demand reaching up to 2.5 million metric tonnes annually, about 44 per cent of supply is imported.
Exporting countries such as Vietnam are prioritising domestic needs, tightening global availability.
"Higher stockpiles mean buying rice at elevated prices amid a strong US dollar and rising logistics costs. Storage of larger reserves also increases risks of spoilage and added operational expenses," he added.
He said diesel shortages and fertiliser disruptions in key producing countries also threaten future harvests.
"As such, Malaysia must prioritise long-term reforms, including boosting domestic production and modernising agriculture, as 'stockpiling is a buffer, not a solution'," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: eSports star Ryzz scores 9As in SPM [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muhammad Faiz Faryzal, better known as Ryzz, is not only making a name for himself in esports but has also achieved outstanding academic results, scoring 9As in the SPM.
The fifth of eight siblings, the student from SMK Seksyen 7 Shah Alam said he began focusing seriously on achieving excellent SPM results last year during Form Four.
"Since last year, I started to really focus on getting excellent results. But I was still playing games.
"I made sure to manage my time properly for SPM. I prepared a schedule and focused on studying, maybe about two hours a day," Ryzz said in an interview.
He described Mathematics as both his strongest and most challenging subject, but said guidance from his tuition teacher helped him secure an A+.
He admitted that balancing gaming and studies was not always easy, especially as the examinations approached.
"There were times when it felt difficult because sometimes I would study and play, but I had to push myself. Even if it was just for a short while, I made sure to stay disciplined," he said.
On receiving his results, Ryzz said he was both happy and surprised.
"I was very shocked because I did not expect it. I thought I might get a B or B minus, but suddenly I got straight As," he said.
With SPM now behind him, Ryzz said he plans to take some time to relax and focus fully on his gaming career.
"For now, I plan to further my studies in accounting, but before applying to university, I want to focus on esports," he said.
Finally, he advised aspiring young gamers to strike a balance between education and esports.
"Balance your studies and gaming. If you are truly interested in gaming, don't stop completely. You can still study and play at the same time. I believe you can achieve both," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Peer confrontation likely triggered Zara Qairina's distress before death, inquest hears
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir might have been deprived of her usual coping mechanisms after a confrontation with a group of peers at SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha on July 15 last year.
Expert witness Dr Chua Sze Hung testified that the 13-year-old's coping mechanisms largely involved seeking support and interaction with her peers, writing in diaries and, to some extent, non-suicidal self-injury.
"On the night of the incident, after the confrontation at room A-3-6, I believe she was deprived of her usual coping mechanisms," he said before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 63rd day of the inquest into her death.
The 67th witness added that, based on the reconstruction of the incident, multiple witnesses confirmed that Zara was in severe distress when she returned to her room at A-3-5.
"One of the witnesses said she appeared the saddest that night.
"The deceased cherished interaction with her peers. On that night, after the confrontation, for various reasons, her peers did not come to console her. That would have had a significant psychological impact on her."
Deputy public prosecutor and coordinating officer Mohd Fairuz Johari also examined whether the confrontation could have been the sole factor that led to Zara being found on the ground floor of the school dormitory the following day.
Dr Chua said he did not believe the incident was a single factor.
"The suicide could have occurred at another time under different circumstances and other destabilising factors, but it is unlikely it would have occurred that night or the following morning without the confrontation at A-3-6.
"However, the confrontation at A-3-6 was definitely the triggering and precipitating factor."
Meanwhile, Dr Chua said he had covered a sufficient number of key witnesses — about 40 — spanning major aspects of the deceased's life and the circumstances leading to the incident.
He added that a large proportion of the assessment was based on secondary data and collateral information obtained from interviews and document reviews.
"But I do consider the diaries as primary data."
Zara, 13, was found unconscious on the ground floor of SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha on July 16 and was pronounced dead the following day.
The inquest will resume this afternoon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Light at last for Hong Kong's Central office market after seven-year slump
Article:
HONG KONG: Hong Kong's Grade-A office market in the city's financial core is finally hitting a floor after nearly seven years of decline, buoyed by a roaring capital market that is boosting demand from both mainland Chinese and multinational firms, realtors said.
The turnaround follows a blockbuster IPO year in 2025, when funds raised in Hong Kong jumped 231 per cent to US$37 billion. With another crowded pipeline shaping up this year, the momentum is spilling beyond trading floors and into prime office towers.
"Companies are coming because of the capital markets, definitely," said Sam Gourlay, JLL Hong Kong head of office leasing advisory.
Among the top names that have snapped up Hong Kong office space is Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com, which in December bought a 50 per cent stake in a tower in the Central business district for US$450 million.
In neighbouring Causeway Bay, Alibaba, with its fintech affiliate Ant Group, bought 13 floors of Mandarin Oriental's new flagship office tower for US$925 million. The listing boom has also breathed life into Central's leasing market. Financial institutions IMC Group and Northern Trust rented new spaces this year, following Jane Street letting six floors in a soon-to-be-completed office tower in June in one of the largest Central leasing deals in decades.
RECOVERY REMAINS UNEVEN
Still, realtors and analysts warned the recovery remains uneven, with substantial oversupply elsewhere continuing to drag on demand.
"Hong Kong commercial property is undergoing a highly specific recovery," S&P said in a report on Thursday. "This recovery is not broad-based; it is concentrated within select Grade-A office towers and for some landlords adept at attracting luxury retail tenants."
Hong Kong's overall office valuations and rents have collapsed by more than 50 per cent since 2019, hitting cash flows at debt-laden developers, forcing some into restructurings and leaving banks nursing heavy charges on soured commercial property loans.
Since the second half of 2025, transacted prices in Central's Grade A offices rose around 5.0 per cent, realtors said.
Rents rose 3.5 per cent in the first two months of 2026, with the vacancy rate improving for the third consecutive month in February to 9.9 per cent, according to data by JLL. That compared to the city's overall rate of 13.4 per cent, and Kowloon East district's 19.5 per cent..
CHINESE FIRMS EYE PRIME OFFICE SPACE
Property agents said more Chinese companies, including tech firms, are now looking to buy office space in the financial district, aligning with the city's push to position itself as a regional tech hub.
CBRE Hong Kong head of capital markets Reeves Yan said more local capital is increasingly moving into strata-office investments, wagering on a sector recovery, while foreign funds and corporate buyers remain largely absent.
"We have passed the peak of oversupply in Central, which was 2024-25," said Yan, adding that new supply in the next five years will likely be half of the past five years.
"Most of the new spaces have been filled up, and now it's become a landlord market where people are fighting for high-quality offices."
In leasing, on the other hand, expansion was led by international financial institutions, market data showed, while Chinese securities firms accounted for more deals but at smaller sizes.
The hedge fund sector occupies less than 2.0 per cent of total office space in Hong Kong yet accounted for 18 per cent of all net demand last year, said JLL's Gourlay.
In the first three months this year, some of the major new leases in the city also came from J.P. Morgan, law firm Ropes & Gray, as well as Chinese securities firms Futu and E Fund.
"I wouldn't be surprised if this year certainly matches last year in numerical transactions and the strength of the hedge funds and asset managers," Gourlay said. "We have multiple conversations at the moment. Nearly all of them in that sector are expanding - and aggressively."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bloomberg Malaysia seeks to strike out Bestinet libel suit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bloomberg (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd is seeking to have a libel suit struck out, arguing it had no role in publishing an article that linked Bestinet Sdn Bhd and its founder, Datuk Seri Aminul Islam Abdul Nor, to a migrant worker recruitment syndicate.
In a High Court filing, Bloomberg (Malaysia), through lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, described the plaintiffs' statement of claim dated Feb 12, as "scandalous, frivolous, and vexatious" and an abuse of the court process.
In the document, it stressed that it did not publish, upload, or disseminate the impugned article in January and does not operate or control the website on which it appeared.
It said that the Bloomberg website clearly states it is run by Bloomberg LP in New York, not by its Malaysian arm.
The suit names 10 defendants, including Bloomberg LP, Bloomberg (Malaysia), The Edge Communications Sdn Bhd, Mnow Media Sdn Bhd, Mkini Dotcom Sdn Bhd, Norhafizan Muhamad and  Nur Azwan Nusi of Harapan Media Enterprise, lawyer Latheefa Koya, Pandan member of parliament Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli and activist Iswardy Morni.
During case management today, High Court judge Roslan Mat Nor fixed June 26 to hear the application by Bloomberg (Malaysia).
Bestinet and Aminul Islam are represented by lawyer N. Ravindran.
The judge also instructed Ravindran to update the court on the status of the service of writ of summons to Bloomberg LP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: War intensifies as Iran vows 'crushing' strikes on US and Israel
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran on Thursday threatened "crushing" attacks on the US and Israel, firing missiles at Tel Aviv after US President Donald Trump vowed to bomb the Islamic republic "back to the Stone Ages."
The war, which erupted more than a month ago with US-Israeli strikes on Iran, has spread throughout the Middle East and roiled the global economy, impacting hundreds of millions worldwide.
In a prime-time White House address, Trump said the US was "very close" to achieving its objectives but warned attacks would intensify if Iran did not reach a negotiated settlement.
"Over the next two to three weeks, we are going to bring them back to the Stone Ages, where they belong," said Trump in a 19-minute speech delivered in front of American flags.
Iran's response was immediate, with Israeli air defences pressed into action and police responding to "several" impact sites, as four people were reportedly lightly injured in the Tel Aviv area.
The Iranian military command centre Khatam Al-Anbiya put out a statement carried on state TV warning the US and Israel to expect "more crushing, broader, and more destructive actions."
"With trust in Almighty God, this war will continue until your humiliation, disgrace, permanent and certain regret, and surrender," said the statement.
The latest attacks came as Jewish Israelis were celebrating Passover, which some were forced to do underground.
"This is not my first choice," said a writer who gave his name as Jeffrey, at a meal organised in a bunker in Tel Aviv.
"But at least in the shelter, we can sit here and just ride it out," he added.
- 'RESIST UNTIL THE END' -
Trump has recently raised the possibility of a deal to end the war, which has pushed up fuel prices in the US and around the world, and pushed down his approval ratings.
He said talks could be possible with Iran's new leadership, which he described as "less radical and much more reasonable" than their predecessors.
But Teheran has dismissed Washington's ceasefire overtures, describing US demands to end the conflict as "maximalist and irrational."
"Messages have been received through intermediaries, including Pakistan, but there is no direct negotiation with the US," said Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei, quoted by the ISNA news agency on Thursday.
Trump warned that if no agreement with Teheran was struck, Washington had "our eyes on key targets including the country's electric generating plants."
Pro-government Iranians voiced defiance as they marched in Teheran at the funeral of a Revolutionary Guards naval commander killed in an Israeli strike.
"This war has lasted a month. However long it takes, we will continue," said Moussa Nowruzi, a 57-year-old pensioner.
"We will resist until the end."
In Lebanon, Hizbollah said its fighters launched drones and rockets at northern Israel Thursday, with the Israeli military's Home Front Command saying air raid sirens were activated.
A day earlier, Israel killed a top Hizbollah commander, two sources told AFP, in a Beirut strike that the Lebanese health ministry said killed seven people.
Authorities in Lebanon say Israeli attacks have killed more than 1,300 people in the country since war erupted between Israel and Iran-backed Hizbollah on March 2.
- 'OXYGEN FOR VOLATILITY' -
The conflict has drawn in Gulf countries once seen as a safe haven in a volatile region, with air defences in the United Arab Emirates responding to missile and drone "threats" Thursday.
Trump vowed the United States would not allow allies in the region – Israel, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain – to "get hurt or fail in any way, shape or form."
The war has highlighted the importance of the Strait of Hormuz, a shipping lane through which one-fifth of the world's oil usually passes.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards have vowed to keep it shut to the country's "enemies" while Trump has made reopening it a condition for a ceasefire.
Britain will lead a summit of 35 countries later Thursday to discuss how to restore freedom of navigation in the strait.
Trump's speech did nothing to reassure markets, as oil prices spiked and stocks tumbled.
The message was "unmistakably one of unfinished business", said Stephen Innes, analyst at SPI Asset Management.
"And in markets, unfinished business is oxygen for volatility."
The World Bank's Managing Director Paschal Donohoe told AFP his institution was "extremely concerned" about the war's impact on inflation, jobs and food security.
The real world economic impact is being felt around the globe, with airlines in China saying they will hike fuel surcharges and Malaysian civil servants being asked to work from home.
Even the Himalayan kingdom of Bhutan is feeling the impact, with the government saying "external conditions beyond our control" had forced a hike in fuel prices.
AFP reporters in the capital Thimphu saw long queues at filling stations Thursday, with shortages hitting the landlocked nation of around 800,000 people.
"I don't know what to say. It's not like our government is responsible, they are trying their best despite the war in the Middle East and price hike in India," said Karma Kalden, 40, a resident of Thimphu.
"We are helpless."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rule of law must reach the poorest, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed the government's commitment to strengthening access to justice, placing it at the core of ongoing legal and institutional reforms.
He said a core tenet of the rule of law is that the same set of laws must be accessible to and enforceable on all, stressing that upholding this principle is as vital within nations as it is across the international community.
"Too often, it is the poorest and most marginalised among us who are deprived of meaningful recourse to the law.
"It is for this reason that my administration has made access to justice a central pillar of our broader reform agenda.
"But we recognise, of course, that much work lies ahead. The ultimate goal is a point where there is access to justice for every citizen, such that no one is left without a remedy," he said in his speech at the opening ceremony of the Commonwealth Legal Education Association Conference 2026 at Universiti Malaya here.
He said access to justice has been made a central pillar of the government's broader reform agenda, particularly in addressing disparities faced by vulnerable groups.
"All this, in the struggle to ensure the continuity of justice, reflects what we are advancing in Malaysia through the ethos of our civilisational development," he said.
He stressed that the law underpins the day-to-day lives of citizens and must not be confined to an exclusive domain that renders it inaccessible to the public, reminding both current and future practitioners to ensure it serves the public good.
"Lawyers must be more than just experts in legal minutiae such as research and drafting.
"They must be ethical authorities, moral anchors, ensuring that what is correct is also just. They must also have the empathy to navigate the human trauma that lies beneath legal disputes.
"It is necessary to comprehend the needs of the public and not to gatekeep the law within a gilded cage that makes it inaccessible to the common man," he added.
Also present were Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Minister Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, and Universiti Malaya pro-chancellor Tan Sri Datuk Zainun Ali.
Earlier, Anwar opened the conference, which brings together legal practitioners, academics, policymakers, regulators and legal educators from across the Commonwealth and other jurisdictions to deliberate on contemporary developments and emerging challenges in legal education and practice.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing family of six found in Bentong
Article:
KUANTAN: A woman and her five children, who were previously reported missing since Monday, were found safe after they came to the Bentong police headquarters last night.
Bentong police chief Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar said all involved arrived in safe condition around 9.30pm yesterday.
"Preliminary investigations found that they left home due to family misunderstandings," he said in a statement today.
Zaiham expressed appreciation to all parties, especially Bentong residents, for their cooperation and the information provided, which led to the successful resolution of the case.
Yesterday, the media reported that a family of six was
allegedly missing
after they were believed to have left their home in Taman Orkid, Bentong, on Monday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Witness statement strengthens conclusion on Zara's manner of death
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A witness statement from a student has strengthened a consultant forensic psychiatrist's conclusion on the manner of death of Zara Qairina Mahathir.
Dr Chua Sze Hung, the 67th witness in Zara's inquest, was requested during proceedings to read the statement of the 65th child witness, believed to be the last person to have
interacted with Zara
on July 16.
Deputy public prosecutor and coordinating officer Mohd Fairuz Johari had earlier asked the expert whether the 65th child witness was among the 40 individuals he interviewed for the psychological autopsy report.
However, Dr Chua said the student was not among his interviewees.
After about five minutes of reading the statement, Dr Chua said he maintained his conclusion that the manner of death was suicide.
"The information brings us closer and provides more details of the deceased's mental state leading to the incident.
"In my opinion, a 13-year-old adolescent without intellectual impairment would have understood that 'terjun' would result in certain death," he said, adding that the 65th child witness had also indirectly indicated that the action would lead to death.
Mohd Fairuz also sought clarification on Dr Chua's earlier statements dismissing the possibility that Zara had been physically pushed.
Dr Chua said that upon completing the psychological autopsy, it was clear there were significant predisposing and precipitating factors for suicide.
"I did not come across any information suggesting that the deceased had been pushed.
"I also referred to the forensic pathologist's report, which stated that it was unlikely the deceased had accidentally fallen or been pushed."
The expert added that as the assessment was retrospective and conservative, he did not have the opportunity to interview the deceased.
"Hence, it would be unfair for me to make a diagnosis without sufficient information to meet the criteria for a diagnosable mental illness."
Zara, 13, was found unconscious on July 16 last year on the ground floor of the SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha dormitory and was pronounced dead the following day at Queen Elizabeth Hospital.
The inquest will resume in the afternoon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrix Concepts spotlights MVV 2.0 vision, positions TechValley as key growth catalyst
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has unveiled the broader vision and investment potential of Malaysia Vision Valley City, its future integrated development within Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 (MVV 2.0), with MVV TechValley as a key industrial driver.
During a recent business forum held in collaboration with UOB Malaysia, the property company highlighted MVV 2.0's role as one of the country's key economic growth corridors, alongside insights on global economic trends, financing solutions and market outlook.
Additional perspective on the transformation for Negeri Sembilan under the MVV initiative was shared by Ho Chin Soon Research, reinforcing the region's growing appeal as an investment destination.
In conjunction with the forum, Matrix Concepts announced that the latest phase of semi-detached factories at MVV TechValley Biz Park has achieved more than 85 per cent take-up during its pre-launch phase, signalling strong market confidence.
The 40-unit development, priced between RM3.4 million and RM4.4 million, attracted a diverse mix of buyers from the manufacturing, logistics and technology sectors seeking strategically located industrial space within the MVV corridor.
Matrix Concepts group executive director Kelvin Lee Chin Chuan said that businesses today are looking beyond traditional industrial zones.
"They are prioritising connectivity, ecosystem support and long-term growth potential, and this is where developments like MVV TechValley come into focus," he said.
He added that MVV TechValley continues to attract strong interest due to its strategic connectivity and integration within a broader development vision.
MVV TechValley's appeal is underpinned by its strategic connectivity with direct access to the Nilai-Labu-Enstek Motorway and will benefit from future links to the North-South Motorway.
The development is also located near key logistics gateways such as Kuala Lumpur International Airport and Port Klang, making it well-suited for manufacturing, distribution and export-orientated businesses.
These advantages position the development well for businesses involved in manufacturing, distribution and export-orientated activities.
MVV TechValley is Matrix Concepts' second industrial development following the success of Sendayan TechValley, where all phases were fully taken up. Building on this track record, the development forms part of a broader industrial ecosystem supported by nearby residential communities, commercial centres and supporting infrastructure.
Beyond its industrial component, MVV TechValley sits within the larger vision of MVV City — a future-planned, integrated city where industry, business and community development coexist. The township integrates industrial zones with residential neighbourhoods, commercial hubs and lifestyle amenities, creating an environment designed to support both businesses and the workforce.
Matrix Concepts plans to roll out additional phases of factory developments within the region, including semi-detached and cluster units, to meet evolving business needs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nation in crisis preparedness mode - Zahid
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the country is currently in crisis preparedness mode following global uncertainties which affect fuel prices.
Therefore, Zahid who is also the rural and regional development minister, asked all parties not to point fingers at the government regarding the fuel price hike.
He said the current situation was caused by the global crisis and required the cooperation of all parties to deal with it together.
"We should not feel complacent and only point fingers at the government, especially the prime minister.
"The fact is that it is a global crisis that we must all face together," he told the media after the monthly assembly of the Rural and Regional Development Ministry, here today.
Asked if the opposition continued to play up the fuel price hike issue as political capital, Zahid said that it should not happen since the current situation was a direct result of international geopolitical developments.
"Ask them (the opposition) to go to the Strait of Hormuz now or try going to a West Asian country, (let) them see for themselves.
"We are actually lucky... so don't keep using this global problem as a fault by the government. This is the reality we have to accept," he said.
Zahid asked all parties not to point fingers and to come together to find a solution.
He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is also scheduled to hold a meeting with all menteris besar and chief ministers this evening to clarify the current situation and ensure that the state government understands the challenges facing the country following the global energy crisis.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam: MATF on right track after US$15.8bil recovery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's Multi-Agency Task Force (MATF) is on the right track in combating financial and corporate crimes, having recovered US$15.8 billion (RM64 billion) in illicit proceeds between 2023 and 2025.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said he hopes MATF will surpass the US$15.8 billion recovery figure within the next two years, stressing the need to further strengthen enforcement efforts.
"We must be more effective so we can recover even more within the next one or two years.
"I hope MATF will be able to exceed this amount moving forward," he said on the sidelines of a special engagement session here today.
Azam, who is deputy chairman of MATF, said the achievement reflects an unprecedented level of cooperation among enforcement agencies in tackling offences such as tax evasion and corporate crime.
"This level of recovery has never been achieved before.
"It shows strong collaboration in tackling tax evasion, corporate crime and related offences," he said.
He added that continued coordination and a more aggressive approach among agencies would be key to sustaining the momentum and enhancing recovery outcomes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cheah draws the line on RM11bil IJM deal: "Take it or we walk away" [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: With just days to go before the April 6 deadline, the Sunway Bhd founder and controlling shareholder Tan Sri Jeffrey Cheah has made his position clear: the RM11 billion takeover offer for IJM Corp Bhd is final.
Cheah, who holds a 59.4 per cent stake in Sunway, said the group has already tabled its best and final offer.
"If shareholders do not accept, we will walk away," he told Business Times.
He added that Sunway's growth trajectory remains intact even without the deal, supported by strong pipelines in healthcare, property development and real estate investment.
The group launched its takeover bid on Jan 12, offering RM3.15 each for IJM's 3.51 billion shares through a combination of cash and newly-issued Sunway shares, a move that could reshape Malaysia's construction and infrastructure landscape.
If successful, the merger would create a formidable industry player capable of rivalling Gamuda Bhd, forming what Cheah describes as a "national champion" with the scale to compete both regionally and globally.
The transaction is targeted for completion by the third quarter of 2026.
"Some shareholders may be hoping for more, perhaps a higher price or more cash. But we have already put forward what we believe is a fair and reasonable offer, based on market realities and supported by data," he said.
Notably, the takeover does not require an extraordinary general meeting as it is extended directly to IJM shareholders under Malaysia's takeover rules, placing the final decision squarely in the hands of individual investors.
Cheah emphasised that the proposal goes beyond a typical corporate exercise, positioning it instead as a Malaysian-led consolidation to build a large-scale national champion.
If it proceeds, he said, the combined entity would emerge as a "mega champion" with stronger capabilities to compete across key sectors both domestically and on the global stage.
"The (potential) Sunway-IJM merger is a true Malaysian deal. What ever happens it will be business as usual for Sunway," Cheah said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM to visit Kuantan tomorrow
Article:
KUANTAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to undertake a one-day working visit here tomorrow, focusing on district development initiatives as well as engagement with youth and silat practitioners.
His political secretary, Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi, said the visit will begin with Friday prayers at Masjid Saidina Umar Al-Khattab in Cenderawasih, followed by a meet-and-greet session with local residents.
"After prayers, the prime minister will chair the Kuantan District Development Meeting at the District and Land Office to discuss local development priorities, including efforts to strengthen the district economy and improve public wellbeing," he said in a statement today.
In the afternoon, Anwar is set to attend the Anak Pahang Madani Youth Gathering at the Kuantan UTC compound at 3pm, expected to draw more than 7,000 young people from across the state.
"The gathering will provide a platform for youth in Pahang to build awareness, sharpen critical thinking and play an active role in shaping the nation's future, while also engaging directly with the prime minister to share views and ideas," Ahmad Farhan noted.
He said Anwar will conclude the visit in the evening at the Gathering of Pendekar Madani Pahang at the Pahang Sports Complex (SUKPA), bringing together silat masters, teachers and practitioners from across the state.
He said the programme underscores efforts to elevate silat as a cultural heritage while reinforcing values of discipline, unity and camaraderie.
Ahmad Farhan said the two gatherings reflect a balance between nurturing young minds and strengthening cultural identity and national spirit.
"It also highlights the Madani Government's commitment to empowering youth as future national assets while preserving cultural heritage so it remains vibrant and relevant in line with the Malaysia Madani framework," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Seven witnesses expected in Mindef drone trespass case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Seven prosecution witnesses are expected to testify in the trial of two Chinese nationals accused of trespassing into a restricted zone at the Defence Ministry.
On Aug 15 last year, then Wangsa Maju police chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohammad Lazim Ismail said the two were caught by Military Police while operating a drone on a football field near Wisma Pertahanan.
The accused, photographers Li Luorong, 28, and Chen Junhao, 21, will stand trial after the Attorney-General's Chambers rejected their bid for a reduced charge.
The duo were brought to court for case management today and appeared calm in the dock.
Suhaila fixed a four-day trial on April 29 and 30 and continuing on June 3 and 4.
She is expected to deliver her decision on the prosecution's case on June 30.
Li and Chen are represented by defence lawyer Ooi Hong Ming.
They were denied bail after they were charged last year.
Li is currently held at Sungai Buloh Prison while Chen is detained at the Puncak Alam Correctional Centre as he is a minor.
On the first charge, Li and Chen are jointly accused of flying a DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise drone to capture aerial footage in a gazetted prohibited area without authorisation, with the intent of acquiring official information detrimental to national security.
The offence allegedly occurred at 11.50am at the ministry's Padang Bola B in Jalan Padang Tembak on Aug 6 last year.
The charge, framed under Section 3(a) of the Official Secrets Act read with Section 34 of the Penal Code, carries life imprisonment upon conviction.
Section 3(a) deals with any person who, for any purpose prejudicial to the safety or interest of Malaysia, approaches, inspects, passes over, or is in the neighbourhood of, or enters any prohibited place.
On the second charge, the duo are accused of using the same drone to capture aerial footage for surveying purposes without obtaining prior authorisation from the authorities at the same time, location and date.
The charge under Section 4(1) of the Act read with Section 34 of the Code provides for a jail term of between one year and 14 years upon conviction.
On Aug 15 last year, Wangsa Maju police chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohammad Lazim Ismail said the two were caught by Military Police while operating a drone on a football field near Wisma Pertahanan.
Lazim said preliminary investigations revealed that the pair were instructed by their employer to record drone footage at the site but were unaware of the actual purpose.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam Baki confirms no MACC link in 'corporate mafia' probe
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Tan Sri Azam Baki has reiterated that police have found no involvement of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) officers or its leadership in the ongoing investigation into alleged "corporate mafia" activities.
The MACC chief commissioner said the matter has been fully handed over to the police and declined to comment further, stressing that he did not wish to interfere with ongoing investigations.
"I do not wish to comment on the matter as we have left it entirely to the police and do not want to disrupt the investigation.
"The police themselves have issued a statement confirming that this
has no connection with MACC.
That is all. I do not wish to comment further," he told reporters after delivering a keynote address at the Multi-Agency Task Force (MATF): Way Forward engagement session here today.
It was previously reported that police investigations into the alleged "corporate mafia" case involving businessman Victor Chin have found no links to MACC personnel.
Bukit Aman's Anti-Money Laundering (Amla) investigation team has, since October last year, recorded statements from 41 individuals, none of whom were MACC officers or senior personnel.
This was confirmed by Bukit Aman Amla investigation team chief Datuk Fazlisyam Abd Majid, who said no MACC personnel had been called to assist in the probe.
He declined further comment as investigations are ongoing.
Meanwhile, Chin is believed to have left the country a few days after being released by police in December last year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Intel looks to put millions more into SambaNova startup chaired by CEO Tan
Article:
SAN FRANCISCO: Chipmaker Intel has planned to invest another US$15 million in SambaNova, a chip startup chaired by Intel chief executive officer Lip-Bu Tan, a Reuters review of corporate records showed.
The investment, subject to regulatory approval, would increase Intel's ownership of SambaNova to 9 per cent, the review showed. The backing follows another US$35 million that Intel put into SambaNova in February, which, along with other financing, had boosted Intel's stake in the startup to 8.2 per cent, from 6.8 per cent last year. The companies announced a "strategic collaboration" in February.
The specifics of this deal and others, previously unreported, show how Intel continues to pursue transactions that may increase the personal fortune of Tan, a wealthy venture capitalist whom the chipmaker hired a year ago to turn the company around.
Intel said in a late March securities filing that there were four unnamed companies whose financings were material to disclose because of their size and benefit to Tan. The company did not say if the list was exhaustive.
Reuters determined details of these investments and, in the case of startup OPAQUE Systems, the value of Intel's stake, by reviewing the chipmaker's disclosure and cross-referencing it with other public statements from Intel and the startups.
The four Tan-affiliated companies are EPIC Microsystems, 3D Glass Solutions, OPAQUE Systems and SambaNova, the review showed.
In a statement, Intel said it"maintains rigorous, well-established governance and conflict-of-interest policies, with active Board oversight to ensure all decisions are made in the best interests of the company and its shareholders."
It added that Intel was already a shareholder in three of the four companies prior to Tan's taking the CEO job. Intel did not comment on SambaNova financing specifically.
"In specialized industries like semiconductors and advanced computing, overlap among long-time investors is expected," the company said.
Reuters reported in December that in at least three instances, Intel had chased deals that would benefit Tan, either by exploring bids for Tan-backed startups - including for SambaNova - or investing in them through its venture arm, Intel Capital.
The late March securities filing by Intel revealed a broader scope to such dealmaking.
Two corporate governance experts told Reuters in the December report that such Intel transactions raised red flags because of the conflicts inherent in dealmaking with Tan's portfolio companies. By contrast, some chip-industry analysts have welcomed Tan's industry relationships, which in Tan's view have made him uniquely able to negotiate deals that benefit all parties, Reuters previously reported.
When asked about Intel's March filing, one of the corporate governance experts, Wharton School professor Daniel Taylor, said he did not see anything inherently wrong with the disclosure.
SAMBANOVA STAKE
Tan had pushed Intel to back SambaNova, a startup struggling to deliver on lofty ambitions to rival Nvidia's AI hardware and software ecosystem.
Tan has been SambaNova's board chair since November 2017. The company laid off 77 people in California in April 2025, and around that time it explored fundraising or going up for sale at a lower valuation, Reuters previously reported.
Late last year, Intel and SambaNova signed a non-binding term sheet for an acquisition that ultimately did not occur. Reuters was unable to determine why.
The challenges notwithstanding, Intel upped its stake in February, and it is looking to put in more. Tan's firms are important backers of SambaNova and could lose millions of dollars if the company fails.
In response to Reuters' inquiry, SambaNova said that 2025 was its strongest, record-breaking year and that the company has shifted its focus to a form of AI computing called inference, referring to the computation required to answer a user's query in a chatbot like OpenAI's ChatGPT, which is in high demand.
SambaNova has "aligned the organization and new financing around this transition" and has introduced a new chip, the company said.
OTHER STARTUP INVESTMENTS
Intel in January invested US$2.3 million in AI startup OPAQUE Systems, giving it a 14 per cent stake valued at about US$41 million, Reuters' review showed.
Venture funds affiliated with Tan, Walden and FactoryHQ Fund, held 17 per cent of the startup before the January funding round. The value of their holdings was about US$46 million after the round.
OPAQUE Systems, Walden and FactoryHQ did not respond to requests for comment.
Intel also put US$3.4 million into EPIC Microsystems for a nearly 5 per cent stake in January, the review showed. Tan-backed venture funds were big investors in the semiconductor company, and Tan's son Andrew is on its board.
Intel Capital likewise put US$8 million over two financings into 3D Glass Solutions since Tan became the chipmaker's CEO. Tan's Walden owned 9.6 per cent after the second financing, the review showed.
Andrew Tan, EPIC Microsystems and 3D Glass Solutions did not respond to requests for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Global energy crisis: Ministry restructures programmes to shield rural communities
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Rural and Regional Development Ministry will restructure development programmes this year by prioritising critical projects to help rural communities cope with rising fuel and energy costs.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the move is not intended to reduce allocations or scale down development scope, but rather to ensure programmes are implemented in a more structured manner with high impact on the people.
Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said the new approach will also focus on boosting income generation among small and medium entrepreneurs, particularly micro-entrepreneurs in rural areas, as part of efforts to address the rising cost of living.
"We expect to achieve savings from several programmes. These savings are not meant to reduce or scale down what has been planned, but to restructure the methods of implementation," he told reporters after the ministry's monthly assembly here today.
He said priority will be given to critical projects for implementation.
"Although this is an internal measure undertaken by the ministry, we are taking this proactive step to ensure priority projects are not neglected, while identifying ways to restructure the implementation of these programmes," he said.
Earlier in his speech, Zahid said the ministry plays a strategic role as the frontline in building rural economic resilience.
"This resilience is not merely about helping people withstand rising costs. True resilience lies in the ability of rural areas to adapt quickly while creating new sources of value so that communities are not continuously exposed to recurring crises," he said.
He added that the ministry should not only function as a programme implementer but also act as a facilitator that coordinates immediate assistance, strengthens rural supply chains and opens up new economic opportunities based on local strengths.
"If the government builds defences at the macro level through subsidies, price controls and fiscal policies, then our role at the ministry is to ensure these measures are effectively delivered and felt at the micro level," he said.
Zahid said ministry must move along three key thrusts - addressing short-term pressures in a targeted and immediate manner, reducing dependence on costs vulnerable to global uncertainties, and building new sources of growth by creating value from existing rural strengths and resources.
"In terms of immediate protection, assistance to smallholders, padi farmers, livestock breeders, fishermen, rural entrepreneurs and vulnerable communities must be better structured, faster and more responsive.
"Input assistance, production incentives and welfare support must be based on field data so that those most affected are prioritised.
"In times of crisis, aid that arrives late often fails to function as real protection," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Army camp assault: Superior allegedly slapped, punched soldiers over cleaning duties
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The army has convened a board of inquiry into an incident at Kem Bentong that left a soldier in critical condition after he was allegedly assaulted by a Junior Non-Commissioned Officer (JNCO) on Monday.
This follows the incident that occurred at the 25th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment (25 RMR) on March 30, where the JNCO in question is believed to have used force against several soldiers.
"Preliminary investigations found that the suspect had slapped and punched the victims in the face and abdomen, allegedly due to dissatisfaction over weapon store cleaning duties assigned to them.
"As a result of the incident, Private Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi lost consciousness and was given emergency treatment at the Armed Forces Medical Centre (PPAT) of 25 RMR before being transferred to Bentong Hospital," it said in a statement.
The army added that the victim is currently receiving intensive care with ventilator support at Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital in Temerloh.
It said six other soldiers did not sustain injuries in the incident and were brought to Bentong Hospital by police after having their statements taken.
"The suspected JNCO has been detained by the police to assist in further investigations," it said.
Yesterday,
police detained an army corporal
to assist in the investigations of the incident.
Bentong police chief Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar said the 37-year-old suspect was arrested about 2.30pm yesterday following a police report lodged by a 49-year-old man, who was the father of one of the victims.
He said initial investigations found that the suspect allegedly assaulted a 24-year-old victim by hitting, punching and kicking him on several parts of the body.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Toxic beauty: MOH bans two cosmetic products over mercury and other poisons
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) no longer permits the sale of two cosmetic products, Glowing Nite Treatment and Glowing Herbal Treatment, which have been found to contain scheduled poisons.
It said in a statement today that the two products contained prohibited substances such as mercury, hydroquinone, tretinoin and betamethasone 17-valerate.
“Following that, the notifications for the involved cosmetics have been revoked by the Director of Pharmacy Services and are no longer allowed to be sold in Malaysia,” according to the statement.
The MOH said that mercury is banned in cosmetic products because the substance can be absorbed into the body and cause damage to the kidneys and nervous system, in addition to potentially disrupting the brain development of children, including unborn babies.
“Mercury can also cause rashes, irritation and other changes to the skin,” said the statement.
The ministry said that substances such as betamethasone 17-valerate, hydroquinone, and tretinoin are medications that must be registered with the Drug Control Authority and can only be used under the advice of a healthcare professional.
The MOH said the use of cosmetics containing these substances without professional health supervision can cause various side effects. It includes thinning of the facial skin, irritation, acne, pigmentation changes, and an increased risk of the substance being absorbed into the bloodstream, which can lead to harmful effects.
“These cosmetic sellers and distributors need to stop the sale and distribution of these cosmetics immediately,” said the statement.
The MOH also said that the sale and distribution of these cosmetics are in violation of the Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations 1984.
Individuals who commit offences under these regulations may be subjected to a fine not exceeding RM25,000 or imprisonment not exceeding three years or both for the first offence, and a fine not exceeding RM50,000 or imprisonment not exceeding five years or both for subsequent offences.
For companies that commit offences, they can be fined up to RM50,000 for the first offence and up to RM100,000 for subsequent offences. — Bernama

Title: Power outage stalls Ampang/Sri Petaling line, Rapid Rail rolls out shuttle buses
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Train services on the Ampang/Sri Petaling route are experiencing delays due to a power outage between Awan Besar Station and Sri Petaling Station.
Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd (Rapid Rail) in a statement today said that due to the delay, passengers are expected to experience longer waiting times at the platforms, especially between Bandar Tasik Selatan Station and Awan Besar Station.
In this regard, Rapid Rail has activated alternative train services and free shuttle buses to enable users to continue their journey.
“Alternative train services and free shuttle buses are provided to connect the affected stations,” the statement said.
For alternative train services, trains from Putra Heights Station will turn back at Kinrara BK5 Station with an expected frequency of seven minutes, while trains from Sentul Timur Station will turn back at Bandar Tasik Selatan Station with a frequency of 10 minutes.
In addition, shuttle trains are provided between Bandar Tasik Selatan Station and Awan Besar Station and between Kinrara BK5 Station and Awan Besar Station with an expected frequency of 15 minutes.
Free shuttle bus services are also provided between Bandar Tasik Selatan Station, Sungai Besi, Bukit Jalil and Sri Petaling with a frequency of between 15 to 20 minutes depending on the current traffic conditions.
Rapid Rail said the engineering team is currently on site to carry out repair work to restore normal operations as soon as possible.
To ensure the safety and smooth movement of passengers, auxiliary police officers and station staff have been placed on the platform and station concourse.
Passengers are also advised to seek alternative transportation and refer to Rapid KL’s social media pages and the MyRapid PULSE application for the latest operational information. — Bernama

Title: Rider’s leg severed in early morning KL crash, Honda City driver arrested
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — A 24-year-old motorcyclist had his leg severed in a horrific crash after a car allegedly ran a red light along Jalan Sultan Ismail here early this morning. A second rider was also seriously injured.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Zamzuri Isa said the collision occurred at about 4.27am,
Free Malaysia Today
reported..
The two motorcyclists were crossing the junction on a green light when they were struck by a Honda City.
